summary: Backend-only testing for Single Window Services Platform. Created pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covering health, auth (register/login/me/update), service catalog, user requests (create with base64 file, list-mine without content, owner-only get with content, 403 cross-user, 404 missing, admin can read any), and admin endpoints (list with filters, status updates reflected to user, stats counters, validation 422, non-admin 403). 22/22 tests passed.
critical_code_review_comments: CORS uses allow_origins=['*'] together with allow_credentials=True; browsers reject this combo. Since auth is Bearer-token (not cookies), this is functionally fine but the credentials flag is misleading and should be set to False, or origins should be restricted to the Expo origin if cookies are introduced later.
JWT_SECRET is committed in /app/backend/.env. For production, generate per-environment and inject via secret manager.
GET /api/requests/mine returns up to 500 items, /api/admin/requests up to 1000 — no pagination. Acceptable for MVP, but flag for future.
register/login do not strip phone whitespace before duplicate check on register beyond .strip() applied after the duplicate query — minor edge case.
seed_data_creation: Tests register two TEST_ users per run (TEST_<hex> and TEST_other_<hex>) and create one service request. Data is left in DB; admin can clear if needed. Admin user is auto-seeded by backend startup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public base URL is read from EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL (frontend/.env key is EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL, not EXPO_BACKEND_URL). Admin creds: admin@swsp.uz / Admin@123. Auth is Bearer-token (no cookies). Mongo IDs are uuid strings; _id is excluded server-side. /api/requests serializer strips file content from list and creation responses; full content only via GET /api/requests/{id} for owner or admin.
